description:
Single user mode is used for recovery when the system detects an
issue during boot or by manual selection from the bootloader.
rationale:
Requiring authentication in single user mode prevents an unauthorized
user from rebooting the system into single user to gain root privileges
without credentials.
ocil_clause:
Perform the following to determine if a password is set for the root user: 
# grep -Eq '^root:\$[0-9]' /etc/shadow || echo "root is locked"
No results should be returned. Otherwise, run the following command and follow the prompts to set a password for the root user: 
# passwd root
title: Ensure Authentication Required for Single User Mode
